name: Cloud-Native Microservices
description: A cloud-native microservices architecture with API gateway, service mesh, and independently deployable services. Uses containerization and orchestration platforms like Kubernetes, with clear separation between external traffic, application logic, and data persistence layers.

zones.name: external
zones.description: All traffic originating from outside the organizational boundary, including end-user devices, partner integrations, and public internet sources.

zones.name: dmz
zones.description: The demilitarized zone handling ingress traffic. Contains edge components that terminate external connections, enforce rate limits, and perform initial request validation before forwarding to internal services.

zones.name: application-tier
zones.description: The core application tier containing business logic microservices. Services communicate via service mesh with mTLS, using asynchronous messaging for decoupled operations. Each service runs in isolated containers with defined resource boundaries.

zones.name: data-tier
zones.description: The data persistence layer containing all stateful components. Access is restricted to application-tier services only, with encryption at rest enforced and connection pooling to limit exposure.

zones.name: management
zones.description: The management and operations plane containing infrastructure tooling, observability stack, secret vaults, and deployment pipelines. Strictly access-controlled with privileged credentials.

boundaries.name: Application Tier to Management
controls: Audit trail for all management operations

mermaid_template:
flowchart TB
    subgraph external["External (Untrusted)"]
        user[End User / Browser]
        mobile[Mobile Client]
        thirdparty[Third-Party API]
    end
    subgraph dmz["DMZ (Semi-Trusted)"]
        waf[WAF / DDoS Protection]
        gw(API Gateway)
        lb(Load Balancer)
    end
    subgraph app["Application Tier (Trusted)"]
        svc1(Service A)
        svc2(Service B)
        svc3(Service C)
        mq([Message Queue])
        cache[(Cache)]
    end
    subgraph data["Data Tier (Highly Trusted)"]
        db1[(Database A)]
        db2[(Database B)]
        store[(Object Storage)]
    end
    subgraph mgmt["Management (Highly Trusted)"]
        monitor(Monitoring)
        vault(Secret Management)
        cicd(CI/CD Pipeline)
    end
    user -->|HTTPS/TLS 1.3| waf
    mobile -->|HTTPS/TLS 1.3| waf
    thirdparty -->|HTTPS| waf
    waf --> lb
    lb --> gw
    gw -->|JWT Auth| svc1
    gw -->|JWT Auth| svc2
    gw -->|JWT Auth| svc3
    svc1 <-->|mTLS| svc2
    svc2 <-->|mTLS| svc3
    svc1 --> mq
    mq --> svc3
    svc1 --> cache
    svc1 -->|SQL/TLS| db1
    svc2 -->|SQL/TLS| db2
    svc3 -->|S3 API| store
    svc1 -.->|mTLS| monitor
    svc1 -.->|mTLS| vault
    cicd -.->|Deploy| app
